mission.title: istio: K8s: Using common labels with namespace 'app.kubernetes.io'
mission.description: K8s: Using common labels with namespace 'app.kubernetes.io' . Requested by 34+ users.

steps.title: Check current istio deployment
steps.description:
Verify your istio version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n istio -l app.kubernetes.io/name=istio
helm list -n istio 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working istio installation.

steps.title: Review istio configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant istio configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n istio -l app.kubernetes.io/name=istio
kubectl get configmap -n istio -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=istio
```
There are [common labels](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/common-labels) for K8s and Helm [recommends](https://github.com/helm/helm/blob/master/docs/chart_best_practices/labels.md) using these:

-

steps.title: Apply the fix for K8s: Using common labels with namespace 'app.kubernetes.io'
steps.description:
Apply the configuration change to resolve the issue:
```yaml
istio_labels:
  app_label_name: "app.kubernetes.io/name"
  version_label_name: "app.kubernetes.io/version"
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n istio -l app.kubernetes.io/name=istio
kubectl get events -n istio --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "K8s: Using common labels with namespace 'app.kubernetes.io' " is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: See the linked issue and PR for the community-verified solution.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with istio installed or the issue environment reproducible.
